Title: FLS2-RBOHD-PIF4 Module Regulates Plant Response to Drought and Salt Stress

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Compared with WT, fls2 mutants show decreased sensitivity to drought and salt stress, paralleling their reduced sensitivity to immune stress. Transcriptome analyses indicate that under normal conditions, the effect of the fls2 mutation on the WT transcriptome resembles that of drought treatment, suggesting that FLS2 contributes to drought signal perception and downstream transcriptome reprogramming.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. fls2 mutants are less sensitive to drought and salt stress than WT, showing reduced growth inhibition and leaf damage under these stresses.
2. FLS2 contributes to regulation of ROS and proline accumulation under drought and salt stress, with fls2 mutants displaying reduced H2O2 accumulation and altered proline levels compared with WT.
3. RNA-seq reveals that FLS2-mediated signaling broadly affects the transcriptome under drought and salt, with fls2 effects under control conditions resembling WT under drought.
4. FLS2-mediated signals regulate PIF4 expression under drought and NaCl, placing FLS2 upstream of PIF4 in the FLS2–RBOHD–PIF4 stress-response module.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of leaves from WT, fls2, and rbohd rbohf mutants under control, drought, and NaCl conditions (Illumina HiSeq 2500/HiSeq X Ten). DEGs and TF regulatory networks downstream of FLS2 were identified, with data available under NCBI submission SUB8234436.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FLS2 positively regulates plant sensitivity to drought and salt stress; fls2 mutants exhibit reduced growth inhibition and leaf damage under drought and NaCl compared with WT, and show altered induction of stress-responsive genes and PIF4 under these conditions.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Upon stress perception, FLS2-mediated signaling contributes to ROS and Ca2+ signaling and modulates PIF4 expression, thereby influencing transcriptome reprogramming and physiological responses (proline accumulation, ROS balance) under drought and salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Component of pattern-triggered immunity and abiotic stress signaling; functions upstream in the FLS2–RBOHD–PIF4 module that integrates immune ROS signaling with transcriptional control of drought and salt stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: FLS2 --(activation of downstream signaling under drought and NaCl)--> PIF4 (via modulation of PIF4 transcript levels and its position as a major TF node in the drought-responsive network).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pattern-recognition receptor kinase for the bacterial flagellin peptide flg22, functioning as an immune signal receptor at the cell surface.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil drought (water withheld until soil water content ~10%), 100 mM NaCl watering, and growth in standard 1/2 MS medium; immune-related flg22 is discussed for FLS2 signaling but abiotic phenotypes were assayed mainly under drought and NaCl.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought and salt) and innate immune signaling integration via FLS2-dependent pathways.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress response (drought tolerance, salt tolerance); integration with biotic stress/innate immune signaling.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under drought and salt stress, fls2 mutants accumulate less H2O2 than WT and display altered proline accumulation patterns (lower than WT under drought, higher than WT under salt), and show increased cell death compared with WT under stress.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana WT plants and fls2 T-DNA insertion mutants (SALK_099606 and SALK_141277) grown on 1/2 MS medium and then in soil under controlled growth-chamber conditions.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Soil-based drought treatment by withholding water until soil water content reached ~10% and NaCl treatment by watering with 100 mM NaCl; growth phenotyping and leaf area measurements; DAB and NBT staining for H2O2 and O2−; trypan blue staining for cell death; proline content assay; RNA extraction and Illumina RNA-seq, DESeq differential expression, GO enrichment and TF network construction; qRT-PCR of PIF4.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional analysis of T-DNA insertion fls2 mutants (SALK_099606, SALK_141277) under drought and NaCl soil treatments; phenotyping of growth and leaf area; ROS (O2− and H2O2) and cell-death staining; proline quantification; RNA-seq-based differential expression and GO analysis; comparison of PIF4 expression in WT versus fls2 under drought and NaCl.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Reverse-genetic analysis using previously characterized FLS2 T-DNA insertion mutants from ABRC combined with RNA-seq-based identification of downstream differentially expressed genes and TF regulatory networks under drought and salt treatments.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Loss-of-function fls2 mutants show reduced sensitivity and increased tolerance to drought and salt stress, indicating that down-regulation of FLS2-mediated signaling could be used to enhance plant tolerance to combined abiotic stresses, although potential trade-offs with pathogen immunity must be considered.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Under normal growth conditions, rbohd rbohf double mutants already grow more slowly than WT and exhibit activation of genes associated with salt response, drought response, oxidative stress, hypoxia, JA signaling, and innate immune response, indicating that defective RBOHD-dependent H2O2 production perturbs ROS homeostasis and leads to a chronic stress-like transcriptional state.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. rbohd rbohf double mutants are strongly hypersensitive to drought and salt stress, with severely inhibited growth and leaf yellowing compared with WT.
2. RBOHD-dependent H2O2 production is impaired in rbohd rbohf mutants under both control and stress conditions, correlating with increased cell death and altered proline accumulation.
3. Under normal and stress conditions, rbohd rbohf mutants exhibit extensive transcriptome reprogramming, including constitutive activation of genes associated with salt and drought responses, JA signaling, oxidative stress, hypoxia, and innate immune responses.
4. GO and heatmap analyses indicate that RBOHD-mediated signals are essential for proper regulation of genes involved in plant water-deprivation response and salt stress, supporting a central role for RBOHD in abiotic stress adaptation within the FLS2–RBOHD–PIF4 module.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq profiling of WT, fls2, and rbohd rbohf leaves under control, drought, and NaCl conditions identified the largest number of DEGs in comparisons involving rbohd rbohf, highlighting RBOHD as a major regulator of NaCl-induced transcriptome reprogramming (data under NCBI submission SUB8234436).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RBOHD is essential for appropriate ROS (H2O2) production and transcriptome reprogramming under drought and salt stress; rbohd rbohf double mutants show reduced growth, impaired H2O2 accumulation, increased cell death, and strong hypersensitivity to drought and NaCl, with constitutive and exaggerated activation of abiotic and oxidative stress-related genes.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: RBOHD-dependent H2O2 production modulates ROS homeostasis and is required for appropriate activation and repression of genes responsive to drought, salt, hypoxia, oxidative stress, JA, and immune signals; loss of RBOHD results in defective H2O2 accumulation and exaggerated activation of stress-response pathways.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Key ROS-generating component of the FLS2–RBOHD–PIF4 module; functions downstream of FLS2 in immune and abiotic stress signaling to generate H2O2, which in turn contributes to transcriptome reprogramming and regulation of PIF4 and other stress-related genes under drought and salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: FLS2 --(activation of ROS signaling)--> RBOHD-dependent H2O2 production; RBOHD --(activation under drought and NaCl)--> PIF4 expression and broad sets of stress-responsive genes involved in drought, salt, oxidative stress, hypoxia, JA signaling, and innate immune responses.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Respiratory burst oxidase homolog D, a member of the NADPH oxidase (NOX) family that regulates ROS production.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil drought (progressive drying to ~10% soil water content) and 100 mM NaCl watering under controlled conditions, which strongly inhibit rbohd rbohf growth compared with WT, and under which ROS and transcriptome responses were evaluated.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (drought and salt) via ROS signaling and integration with immune pathways.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress response (drought sensitivity, salt sensitivity); ROS homeostasis and oxidative stress response.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: rbohd rbohf double mutants show slower growth than WT under normal conditions, reduced O2− and H2O2 accumulation under control and stress, increased cell death under stress, and markedly higher proline accumulation under both drought and salt treatments.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana WT plants and rbohd rbohf double mutants (CS9558 and CS68522) grown on 1/2 MS medium and subsequently transplanted to soil for drought and NaCl treatments.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Soil drought and 100 mM NaCl treatments; phenotyping of seedling growth and leaf area; DAB and NBT staining to detect H2O2 and O2−; trypan blue staining for cell death; measurement of proline content; RNA-seq of leaves followed by clustering, PCA, Venn analysis, and GO enrichment to assess RBOHD-dependent transcriptome changes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phenotypic analysis of rbohd rbohf T-DNA double mutants (CS9558 and CS68522) under drought and 100 mM NaCl soil treatments; growth and leaf-area measurements; DAB and NBT staining for ROS; trypan blue staining for cell death; proline quantification; RNA-seq and GO enrichment to characterize transcriptome reprogramming under control, drought, and salt conditions.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Use of previously isolated rbohd rbohf T-DNA insertion lines from ABRC to disrupt RBOHD-dependent ROS production, combined with RNA-seq-based detection of downstream differentially expressed genes and TF networks under abiotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: RBOHD-mediated H2O2 production is required to maintain ROS homeostasis and prevent hypersensitivity to drought and salt stress; enhancing or fine-tuning RBOHD activity could help breed plants that better tolerate drought and salinity without constitutive stress activation or growth retardation.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: TF network analysis shows PIF4 as a primary transcription factor in WT under drought versus control, regulated by multiple TFs. Previous work cited by the authors indicates that PIF4 cooperates with WRKY33 to regulate ROS homeostasis under drought and salt stress, consistent with its position downstream of FLS2 and RBOHD.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TF network analysis under drought identifies PIF4 as a primary transcription factor in WT, regulated by multiple other TFs.
2. PIF4pro::GUS reporter plants show strong induction of GUS activity under mannitol and NaCl treatments, indicating stress-inducible promoter activity.
3. PIF4 transcript levels are regulated by FLS2- and RBOHD-mediated signals; under drought PIF4 induction is weaker in fls2 and rbohd rbohf mutants than in WT, and under NaCl PIF4 expression decreases in these mutants.
4. pif4 mutants display increased tolerance to both drought and salt stress compared with WT and exhibit reduced flg22-triggered root growth inhibition, supporting a role for PIF4 as a positive regulator of stress sensitivity and immune responses downstream of FLS2 and RBOHD.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq-based TF network construction under drought and NaCl identified PIF4 as the primary TF node in WT_drought versus WT_CK, indicating extensive regulatory connectivity; this network was built using DEGs from the RNA-seq dataset (NCBI submission SUB8234436).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PIF4 acts as a central transcription factor downstream of FLS2 and RBOHD; pif4 mutants are more tolerant to drought and salt stress, and show reduced inhibition of root elongation by flg22, indicating that PIF4 positively contributes to stress sensitivity and FLS2-dependent immune responses.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: As a transcription factor, PIF4 integrates signals downstream of FLS2- and RBOHD-mediated pathways, modulating ROS homeostasis and the expression of drought- and salt-responsive genes as well as components of the immune response; reduced PIF4 function shifts plants toward increased tolerance to drought and salt and diminished flg22 sensitivity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Central TF in the FLS2–RBOHD–PIF4 module; acts downstream of immune receptor FLS2 and ROS producer RBOHD to regulate transcriptional networks controlling plant responses to drought, salt stress, and pathogen-associated molecular patterns.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: FLS2 --(activation under drought and NaCl)--> PIF4 expression; RBOHD --(activation under drought and NaCl)--> PIF4 expression; PIF4 --(positive regulation)--> flg22-triggered immune responses (root growth inhibition) and transcriptional programs associated with drought and salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PHYTOCHROME INTERACTING FACTOR 4, a phytochrome-interacting transcription factor.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: PIF4pro::GUS expression in seedling cotyledons is strongly induced by mannitol and NaCl treatments compared with untreated control; PIF4 transcript levels are induced by drought in WT and to a lesser extent in fls2 and rbohd rbohf, while under NaCl treatment PIF4 expression decreases in fls2 and rbohd rbohf compared with WT.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mannitol (150 mM) and NaCl (100 mM) in 1/2 MS medium to induce PIF4pro::GUS activity; soil drought and 100 mM NaCl watering to test pif4 stress tolerance; 1 μM flg22 treatment to test FLS2-dependent immune signaling.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Transcriptional regulation of abiotic stress tolerance (drought and salt) and integration with innate immune signaling.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance, salt tolerance); biotic stress response (pattern-triggered immunity via flg22).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: In response to flg22, root elongation of WT seedlings is strongly inhibited, whereas pif4 mutants show only slight inhibition, indicating that PIF4 contributes to FLS2-dependent immune responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana pif4 T-DNA insertion mutant (SAIL_1288_E07) and PIF4pro::GUS (also referred to as PIF4pro::PIF4-GUS) transgenic lines, together with WT plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Growth of PIF4pro::GUS seedlings on 1/2 MS medium with 150 mM mannitol or 100 mM NaCl for 7 days followed by histochemical GUS staining; soil drought and NaCl treatments of pif4 and WT seedlings; qRT-PCR-based measurement of PIF4 expression in WT, fls2, and rbohd rbohf under drought and NaCl; flg22 (1 μM) treatment of seedlings and measurement of root elongation; TF network construction from RNA-seq to identify PIF4 as a central node.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phenotypic analysis of the pif4 T-DNA mutant (SAIL_1288_E07) under drought and 100 mM NaCl soil treatments; root-growth assay in response to 1 μM flg22; expression analysis of PIF4 in WT, fls2, and rbohd rbohf mutants under drought and NaCl; GUS staining of PIF4pro::GUS transgenic seedlings grown on mannitol and NaCl to monitor promoter activity.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: PIF4 was identified from RNA-seq-derived DEGs and TF network analysis as a primary transcription factor regulated under drought and salt stress; functional roles were validated using existing pif4 T-DNA mutants and PIF4pro::GUS reporter lines.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Loss-of-function pif4 mutants display increased tolerance to both drought and salt stress and reduced sensitivity to flg22-triggered root growth inhibition, indicating that down-regulating PIF4 activity could be a promising strategy to improve multi-stress tolerance by modulating ROS homeostasis and stress-responsive transcription networks.
